name: yams
documentation:
# README for yams

## Distributions

The source tarball is available at <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/yams>

## Install

From the source distribution, extract the tarball and run

```
python setup.py install
```

For debian and rpm packages, use your usual tools according to your Linux
distribution.

## Documentation

The documentation is available at <https://yams.readthedocs.io/>

## Code style

The python code is verified against *flake8* and formatted with *black*.

- You can run `tox -e black` to check that the files are well formatted.
- You can run `tox -e black-run` to format them if needed.
- You can include the `.hgrc` to your own `.hgrc` to automatically run black
  before each commit/amend. This can be done by writing `%include ../.hgrc` at
  the end of your `.hgrc`.

## Comments, support, bug reports

See the tracker at <https://forge.extranet.logilab.fr/open-source/yams>

classes.name: yams._exceptions.ValidationError

methods.name: yams._exceptions.ValidationError.translate
documentation:
Translate and interpolate messsages in the errors dictionary, using
the given translation function.

If no substitution has been given, suppose msg is already translated for
bw compat, so no translation occurs.

This method may only be called once.

Validation error details the reason(s) why the validation failed.

Arguments are:

- `entity`: the entity that could not be validated; actual type depends on
  the client library
- `errors`: errors dictionary, None key used for global error, other keys
  should be attribute/relation of the entity, qualified as subject/object
  using {func}`yams.role_name`. Values are the message associated to the
  keys, and may include interpolation string starting with '%(KEY-' where
  'KEY' will be replaced by the associated key once the message has been
  translated. This allows predictable/translatable message and avoid args
  conflict if used for several keys.
- `msgargs`: dictionary of substitutions to be inserted in error
  messages once translated (only if msgargs is given)
- `i18nvalues`: list of keys in msgargs whose value should be translated

Translation will be done **in-place** by calling {meth}`translate`.

documentation: Check that all keys of kwargs are actual attributes.

functions.name: yams.buildobjs.register_base_types
documentation: add base (final) entity types to the given schema

functions.name: yams.buildobjs.make_type
documentation:
create a python class for a Yams base type.

Notice it is now possible to create a specific type with user-defined
behaviour, e.g.:

> Geometry = make_type('Geometry') # (c.f. postgis)

will allow the use of:

> Geometry(geom_type='POINT')

in a Yams schema, provided in this example that `geom_type` is specified to
the {func}`yams.register_base_type` function which should be called prior to
make_type.

functions.name: yams.buildobjs.RichString
documentation:
RichString is a convenience attribute type for attribute containing text
in a format that should be specified in another attribute.

The following declaration:

```
class Card(EntityType):
    content = RichString(fulltextindexed=True, default_format='text/rest')
```

is equivalent to:

```
class Card(EntityType):
    content_format = String(internationalizable=True,
                            default='text/rest', constraints=[FORMAT_CONSTRAINT])
    content  = String(fulltextindexed=True)
```
provides a RichString factory for convenience

classes.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.entities
documentation:
return a list of possible entity's type

If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of types.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.has_entity
documentation: return true the type is defined in the schema

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.eschema
documentation: return the entity's schema for the given type

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.relations
documentation:
return the list of possible relation'types

If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of relation's
types.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.has_relation
documentation: return true the relation is defined in the schema

methods.name: yams.interfaces.ISchema.rschema
documentation: return the relation schema for the given relation type
a schema is a collection of relation and entity schemas
remove this to avoid a dependency on rql for client code
from rql.interfaces import ISchema as IRQLSchema, \
IRelationSchema as IRQLRelationSchema, IEntitySchema as IRQLEntitySchema

classes.name: yams.interfaces.IRelationSchema

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IRelationSchema.associations
documentation:
return a list of (subject_type, [object_types]) defining between
which types this relation may exists

If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of type.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IRelationSchema.subjects
documentation:
return a list of types which can be subject of this relation

If e_type is not None, return a list of types which can be subject of
this relation with e_type as object.
If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of type.
Raise KeyError if e_type is not known

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IRelationSchema.objects
documentation:
return a list of types which can be object of this relation.

If e_type is not None, return a list of types which can be object of
this relation with e_type as subject.
If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of type.
Raise KeyError if e_type is not known.

A relation is a named ordered link between two entities.
A relation schema defines the possible types of both extremities.

classes.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.subject_relations
documentation:
return a list of relations that may have this type of entity as
subject

If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of relation's
types.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.object_relations
documentation:
return a list of relations that may have this type of entity as
object

If schema is not None, return a list of schemas instead of relation's
types.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.subject_relation
documentation:
return the relation schema for the rtype subject relation

Raise KeyError if rtype is not known.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.object_relation
documentation:
return the relation schema for the rtype object relation

Raise KeyError if rtype is not known.

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.relation_definitions
documentation:
return an iterator on "real" relation definitions

"real"  relations are a subset of subject relations where the
object's type is not a final entity

a relation definition is a 2-uple :
* name of the relation
* schema of the destination entity type

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.attribute_definitions
documentation:
return an iterator on attribute definitions

attribute relations are a subset of subject relations where the
object's type is a final entity

an attribute definition is a 2-uple :
* name of the relation
* schema of the destination entity type

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.is_final
documentation:
return true if the entity is a final entity (ie cannot be used
as subject of a relation)

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.constraints
documentation: return the existing constraints on the <rtype> subject relation

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.default
documentation: return the default value of a subject relation

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IEntitySchema.check
documentation:
check the entity and raises an InvalidEntity exception if it
contains some invalid fields (ie some constraints failed)

An entity has a type, a set of subject and or object relations.
The entity schema defines the possible relations for a given type and some
constraints on those relations.

Attributes are defined with relations pointing to a 'final' entity

classes.name: yams.interfaces.IConstraint
documentation: Represents a constraint on a relation.

classes.name: yams.interfaces.IVocabularyConstraint

methods.name: yams.interfaces.IVocabularyConstraint.vocabulary

a specific constraint restricting the set of possible value for an
attribute value

methods.name: yams.reader.SchemaLoader.add_definition

file handler callback to add a definition object

wildcard capability force to load schema in two steps : first register
all definition objects (here), then create actual schema objects (done in
`_build_schema`)

the schema loader is responsible to build a schema object from a
set of files

functions.name: yams.schema.role_name
documentation:
function to use for qualifying attribute / relation in ValidationError
errors'dictionnary

functions.name: yams.schema.rehash
documentation:
this function manually builds a copy of `dictionary` but forces
hash values to be recomputed. Note that dict(d) or d.copy() don't
do that.

It is used to :
  - circumvent Pyro / (un)pickle problems (hash mode is changed
    during reconstruction)
  - force to recompute keys' hash values. This is needed when a
    schema's type is changed because the schema's hash method is based
    on the type attribute. This problem is illusrated by the pseudo-code
    below :

    >>> topic = EntitySchema(type='Topic')
    >>> d = {topic : 'foo'}
    >>> d[topic]
    'foo'
    >>> d['Topic']
    'foo'
    >>> topic.type = 'folder'
    >>> topic in d
    False
    >>> 'Folder' in d
    False
    >>> 'Folder' in d.keys() # but it can be found "manually"
    True
    >>> d = rehash(d) # explicit rehash()
    >>> 'Folder' in d
    True

classes.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.add_subject_relation
documentation: register the relation schema as possible subject relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.add_object_relation
documentation: register the relation schema as possible object relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.relation_definition
documentation:
return a relation definition schema for a relation of this entity type

Notice that when target_type is not specified and the relation may lead
to different entity types (ambiguous relation), one of them is picked
randomly. If also take_first is False, a ValueError exception will be raised.

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.ordered_relations
documentation: return subject relations in an ordered way

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.relation_definitions
documentation:
return an iterator on relation definitions

if include_final is false, only non attribute relation are returned

a relation definition is a 3-uple :
* schema of the (non final) relation
* schemas of the possible destination entity types
* a string telling if this is a 'subject' or 'object' relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.destination
documentation:
return the type or schema of entities related by the given subject relation

`relation_schema` is expected to be a non ambiguous relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.attribute_definitions
documentation:
return an iterator on attribute definitions

attribute relations are a subset of subject relations where the
object's type is a final entity

an attribute definition is a 2-uple :
* schema of the (final) relation
* schema of the destination entity type
attributes description ###########

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.main_attribute
documentation:
convenience method that returns the *main* (i.e. the first non meta)
attribute defined in the entity schema

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.defaults
documentation: return an iterator on (attribute name, default value)

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.default
documentation: return the default value of a subject relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.has_unique_values
documentation:
convenience method to check presence of the UniqueConstraint on a
relation

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.meta_attributes
documentation:
return a dictionnary defining meta-attributes:
* key is an attribute schema
* value is a 2-uple (metadata name, described attribute name)

a metadata attribute is expected to be named using the following scheme:

  <described attribute name>_<metadata name>

for instance content_format is the format metadata of the content
attribute (if it exists).
metadata attributes #############

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.has_metadata
documentation:
return metadata's relation schema if this entity has the given
`metadata` field for the given `attribute` attribute

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.is_metadata
documentation: return a metadata for an attribute (None if unspecified)

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.indexable_attributes
documentation: return the relation schema of attribtues to index
full text indexation control #####

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.fulltext_relations
documentation: return the (name, role) of relations to index

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.fulltext_containers
documentation:
return relations whose extremity points to an entity that should
contains the full text index content of entities of this type

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.is_subobject
documentation:
return True if this entity type is contained by another. If strict,
return True if this entity type *must* be contained by another.
resource accessors ##############

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.check
documentation:
check the entity and raises an ValidationError exception if it
contains some invalid fields (ie some constraints failed)

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.check_value
documentation: check the value of a final entity (ie a const value)

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.convert_value
documentation: check the value of a final entity (ie a const value)

methods.name: yams.schema.EntitySchema.vocabulary
documentation: backward compat return the vocabulary of a subject relation

An entity has a type, a set of subject and or object relations
the entity schema defines the possible relations for a given type and some
constraints on those relations.

classes.name: yams.schema.RelationDefinitionSchema

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationDefinitionSchema.advertise_new_add_permission
documentation:
handle backward compatibility with pre-add permissions

* if the update permission was () [empty tuple], use the
  default attribute permissions for `add`

* else copy the `update` rule for `add`

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationDefinitionSchema.rproperty_defs
documentation:
return a dictionary mapping property name to its definition for each
allowable properties when the relation has `destination_type` as target entity's
type

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationDefinitionSchema.rproperties
documentation:
same as .rproperty_defs class method, but for instances (hence
destination is known to be self.object).

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationDefinitionSchema.check_permission_definitions
documentation: check permissions are correctly defined

a relation definition is fully caracterized relation, eg

<subject type> <relation type> <object type>

classes.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema._init_computed_relation
documentation:
computed relation are specific relation with only a rule attribute.

Reponsibility to infer associated relation definitions is left to client
code defining what's in rule (eg rql snippet in cubicweb).

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.update
documentation: Allow this relation between the two given types schema

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.init_rproperties
documentation: relation definitions properties handling ################################
XXX move to RelationDefinitionSchema

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.associations
documentation:
return a list of (subject, [objects]) defining between which types
this relation may exists

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.subjects
documentation:
Return a list of entity schemas which can be subject of this relation.

If entity_schema is not None, return a list of schemas which can be subject of
this relation with entity_schema as object.

:raise `KeyError`: if entity_schema is not a subject entity type.

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.objects
documentation:
Return a list of entity schema which can be object of this relation.

If entity_schema is not None, return a list of schemas which can be object of
this relation with entity_schema as subject.

:raise `KeyError`: if entity_schema is not an object entity type.

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.targets
documentation: return possible target types with <entity_schema> as <x>

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.relation_definition
documentation: return the properties dictionary of a relation

methods.name: yams.schema.RelationSchema.check_permission_definitions
documentation: check permissions are correctly defined

A relation is a named and oriented link between two entities.
A relation schema defines the possible types of both extremities.

Cardinality between the two given entity's type is defined
as a 2 characters string where each character is one of:
- 1 <-> 1..1 <-> one and only one
- ? <-> 0..1 <-> zero or one
- + <-> 1..n <-> one or more
- * <-> 0..n <-> zero or more
